recommendations.summary: ATI Physical Therapy delivered solid growth in March 2026, with sessions up 6.1% to 107,499 and engaged sessions climbing 8.7% month-over-month, indicating improving content resonance and user intent. Engagement rate improved to 44.9% (+1.1 pp), reflecting better quality traffic overall. Organic Search drove this growth, climbing 11.5% to 64.5k sessions and now commanding 60% of total traffic—a meaningful signal of SEO strength. Custom conversion events show mixed momentum: high-value appointment-related actions (request_an_appointment +10.9%, schedule_an_appointment -5.9%) remain modest in volume, while outbound navigation (outbound_clicks +4.1%, outbound_insurance_clicks +15.7%) and engagement signals (on_page_faq_engagement +11.4%, learn_more_services_cta +8.8%) are accelerating. Notably, store_locate dropped 10.1% and internal_clicks fell 18.1%—both warrant investigation into user navigation friction. Location-level landing pages (Federal Way, Kirkland, East Longmeadow) are emerging as high-growth acquisition channels with engagement rates exceeding 46%, suggesting local search optimization is paying off. YTD through Q1 2026, the property has accumulated 323.7k sessions across three consistent months (Jan: 115k, Feb: 101k, Mar: 107k), establishing a baseline for sustained performance.

quick_wins.title: Capitalize on location landing page momentum
quick_wins.detail: Location pages like /washington/kirkland/11620-97th-ln-ne (+94.5% MoM) and /washington/lynnwood/3105-alderwood-mall-blvd (+50.9%) are driving disproportionate growth with strong engagement (46–58%). Prioritize schema markup, local citation audits, and location-specific keyword optimization for top-performing clinic pages to sustain this lift.

quick_wins.title: Investigate and reverse internal_clicks decline
quick_wins.detail: internal_clicks dropped 18.1% in March despite overall session growth, suggesting navigation or UX friction within the site. Audit internal linking structure, menu hierarchy, and on-page CTAs to identify and fix broken or unclear navigation paths.

quick_wins.title: Test homepage engagement recovery
quick_wins.detail: The homepage (/) saw engagement rate of 65.1% but sessions dropped 9.2% MoM; it remains a critical funnel stage. A/B test hero copy, CTA prominence, and load speed to re-attract direct and referral traffic.

potential_warnings.title: store_locate conversion event declining sharply
potential_warnings.detail: store_locate events dropped 10.1% MoM to 8,760—a key intent signal for users seeking clinic locations. This may indicate friction in the location finder tool, map integration issues, or reduced local search visibility; prioritize auditing the locate tool's UX and performance.

potential_warnings.title: Appointment conversion volume remains low relative to traffic
potential_warnings.detail: request_an_appointment (1,440 events) and schedule_an_appointment (577 events) combined represent <2% of engaged sessions despite strong traffic. This suggests a major drop-off between awareness and conversion; audit form abandonment, mobile responsiveness, and appointment booking friction.

potential_warnings.title: Embed page dominance masking conversion gaps
potential_warnings.detail: The /embed page captures 30% of all sessions but only 20.9% engagement rate—far below site average and below the homepage. This likely reflects embedded content (maps, widgets, iframes on partner sites) with low intent; do not optimize for embed traffic; focus instead on direct and organic visitors.

biggest_opportunities.title: Scale organic search aggressively
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic Search grew 11.5% to 64.5k sessions (60% of traffic mix) and shows the strongest growth velocity. Double down on clinic-level keyword targeting, service-page SEO, and content around physical therapy conditions to capture growing demand; this channel is proven and has room to expand.

biggest_opportunities.title: Unlock appointment conversion funnel
biggest_opportunities.detail: Despite 107k sessions and 48k engaged sessions, appointment requests total only 1,440 events (1.3% conversion). Implement exit-intent CTAs, streamlined appointment forms, telehealth options, and retargeting campaigns on high-traffic pages (/embed, location pages) to convert intent into bookings.

biggest_opportunities.title: Expand and promote FAQ and educational content
biggest_opportunities.detail: on_page_faq_engagement surged 11.4% and outbound_faq_clicks rose 15.2%, signaling strong demand for educational content. Develop clinic-specific FAQ pages, treatment guides, and condition primers for location landing pages and service pages; this builds trust and supports SEO authority.

biggest_opportunities.title: Investigate and revitalize get_directions and locate_me
biggest_opportunities.detail: get_directions declined 2.4% and locate_me grew only 1.6% despite location page growth; these are critical for converting foot traffic. Audit map integrations, directions link accuracy, and mobile UX on clinic pages; ensure Google Business Profile optimization is current.

recommendations.summary: March 2026 saw a significant MoM traffic decline of -29.9% (51,095 sessions vs. 72,904 in February), marking a deceleration from Q1's strong start. However, engagement quality improved materially: engaged sessions grew +23.3% MoM and engagement rate climbed 7.9 percentage points to 18.2%, suggesting that remaining traffic is more qualified. Year-over-year, March 2026 posted +267.6% growth versus March 2025 (13,899 sessions), and the YTD 2026 trajectory remains exceptional at +374.3% (193,679 vs. 40,834 in 2025). Direct traffic dominates at 71.2% of sessions and surged +3443.9% YoY, an anomaly worth auditing for tracking or attribution changes. Custom conversion events show mixed signals: outbound clicks (+4.0% MoM, +29.6% YoY) and store location events (+25.0% MoM, +62.8% YoY) are accelerating, but phone calls declined -9.2% MoM and -35.8% YoY, signaling a potential shift away from voice channels. The homepage (/) demonstrated exceptional performance with 3,587 sessions and 70.1% engagement rate, though it grew primarily on the back of a traffic surge rather than improved conversion funnel performance.

quick_wins.title: Capitalize on homepage momentum
quick_wins.detail: The homepage (/) drove 3,587 sessions with 70.1% engagement rate and +101.3% MoM growth. Ensure apply_now and outbound_click CTAs are prominently placed and optimized to convert this engaged audience.

quick_wins.title: Expand store locator and mobile location features
quick_wins.detail: store_locate (+25.0% MoM, +62.8% YoY) and locate_me (+27.7% MoM, +110.3% YoY) are among the fastest-growing conversion events. Ensure these features are discoverable across all landing pages and mobile-optimized.

quick_wins.title: Investigate and resolve (not set) landing page tracking
quick_wins.detail: 773 sessions attributed to '(not set)' with only 0.8% engagement suggests a tracking gap. Audit GA4 configuration to capture proper landing page values and recover this attribution data.

quick_wins.title: Boost organic search visibility
quick_wins.detail: Organic Search grew +21.7% MoM to 12,157 sessions and remains stable YoY (+0.9%), making it a reliable secondary channel. Allocate SEO budget to high-intent keywords supporting apply_now and store_locate events.

potential_warnings.title: Sharp MoM session decline requires root-cause analysis
potential_warnings.detail: Sessions fell -29.9% from February to March (72,904 → 51,095), despite YoY growth. Direct traffic dropped -41.0% MoM, which may reflect seasonality, campaign wind-down, or a tracking issue given the +3443.9% YoY spike.

potential_warnings.title: Phone call conversions declining sharply
potential_warnings.detail: Calls dropped -9.2% MoM and -35.8% YoY (336 events in March), indicating a potential shift away from voice channels or reduced call-to-action visibility. This is a high-intent conversion event and warrants investigation.

potential_warnings.title: Apply_now conversion down YoY despite traffic growth
potential_warnings.detail: apply_now events declined -19.3% YoY (1,297 in March 2026 vs. ~1,607 estimated in March 2025) despite +267.6% session growth, suggesting conversion funnel leakage or increased bot/low-quality traffic.

potential_warnings.title: Organic Social engagement collapsed YoY
potential_warnings.detail: Organic Social traffic fell -54.4% YoY (272 sessions in March 2026), indicating diminished social channel performance or organic reach decline. This warrants review of social strategy and content distribution.

biggest_opportunities.title: Audit and normalize Direct traffic attribution
biggest_opportunities.detail: Direct jumped +3443.9% YoY to 36,396 sessions (71.2% of traffic), an anomalous growth rate that suggests either a major organic campaign driving direct bookmarks/revisits or a GA4 tracking misconfiguration. Clarifying this is critical for accurate channel strategy.

biggest_opportunities.title: Revitalize phone call channel strategy
biggest_opportunities.detail: Calls declined -35.8% YoY—counter to other conversion events growing. Consider A/B testing click-to-call prominence, SMS integration, or chatbot funneling to reinvigorate phone conversion paths.

biggest_opportunities.title: Optimize low-engagement location landing pages
biggest_opportunities.detail: Pages like /pr/aguadilla/ (13.9% engagement) and /georgia/winder/ (18.6% engagement) attract traffic but fail to engage. Audit for missing store info, apply buttons, or location-specific messaging to lift engagement rates.

biggest_opportunities.title: Scale Paid Search investment
biggest_opportunities.detail: Paid Search grew +68.2% MoM (148 sessions) from a small base and shows promise. Test expanded keyword targeting and higher budgets to capture high-intent users and support apply_now conversions.

recommendations.summary: Advance Financial's March 2026 performance shows a concerning trend: sessions declined 5.6% month-over-month and 17.8% year-over-year to 9,891, while engaged sessions fell even sharper at -10.9% MoM and -46.3% YoY, dragging engagement rate down to 34.5% (down 2.1 percentage points). The YTD 2026 trajectory is worsening—Q1 declined 15.5% versus Q1 2025, and March's -17.8% YoY drop is steeper than both January and February, signaling accelerating headwinds. Direct traffic (51.1% of sessions) fell -10.3% MoM and -19.4% YoY, while organic search lost 27.0% year-over-year despite being stable month-to-month. Paid Search is a bright spot, up 92.7% MoM and +3671.4% YoY, though from a small base of 528 sessions. On the conversion side, most custom events are down sharply YoY—outbound_clicks (-45.6%), store_locate (-56.3%), locate_me (-66.2%), and download_app (-69.4%)—but month-over-month momentum is positive for core application funnels: fasttrack_application_start (+10.0% MoM) and instore_application_start (+8.7% MoM) are bright spots. The homepage (/) remains the strongest landing page with 53.3% engagement rate and growing MoM (+3.8%), while location pages show mixed performance; notably, the Knoxville store page (+22.9% MoM, +9.5% YoY) is a notable standout. The overall picture is one of structural YoY weakness offset by stabilizing MoM trends and emerging Paid Search opportunity.

quick_wins.title: Scale Paid Search immediately
quick_wins.detail: Paid Search delivered 528 sessions in March with exceptional growth (+92.7% MoM, +3671.4% YoY). Increase budget allocation and expand keyword coverage to capitalize on this high-performing channel before competitors saturate it.

quick_wins.title: Investigate Direct traffic decline
quick_wins.detail: Direct traffic (5,055 sessions, 51.1% of total) fell -10.3% MoM and -19.4% YoY; this channel typically signals brand awareness and repeat visitors. Audit branded search campaigns, email list health, and offline marketing attribution to understand the root cause.

quick_wins.title: Optimize Knoxville store page
quick_wins.detail: The Knoxville location page (/tennessee/knoxville/2823-n-broadway) is outperforming: +22.9% MoM and +9.5% YoY, with 107 engaged sessions. Replicate this page's design, CTA strategy, and content to other underperforming location pages.

quick_wins.title: Boost fasttrack_application_start event promotion
quick_wins.detail: fasttrack_application_start grew +10.0% MoM (2,084 events) while overall engagement fell; this suggests qualified intent. Promote this pathway more prominently on homepage and location pages to convert growing interest into applications.

potential_warnings.title: Accelerating YoY session decline
potential_warnings.detail: March 2026 sessions dropped 17.8% YoY versus March 2025 (9,891 vs 12,033), worse than January (-15.9%) and February (-12.8%). This downward trajectory through Q1 indicates a structural traffic acquisition problem, not seasonal noise.

potential_warnings.title: Engagement quality collapsing year-over-year
potential_warnings.detail: Engaged sessions plummeted 46.3% YoY and engagement rate fell 18.2 percentage points YoY to 34.5%. Most high-value conversion events are down sharply YoY: outbound_clicks (-45.6%), store_locate (-56.3%), locate_me (-66.2%), and download_app (-69.4%). This suggests audience quality or relevance has degraded significantly.

potential_warnings.title: Organic Search weakness persists
potential_warnings.detail: Organic Search sessions declined 27.0% YoY to 4,109, representing 41.5% of traffic. While stable MoM (-2.4%), sustained YoY decline indicates SEO visibility or competitive pressure loss that requires immediate keyword and content strategy review.

potential_warnings.title: Email channel near-collapse
potential_warnings.detail: Email sessions dropped to just 2 in March, down 97.9% MoM and representing 0.0% of traffic. This suggests either broken email tracking, depleted list engagement, or campaign suspension that needs immediate investigation.

potential_warnings.title: Blog engagement flat-lining
potential_warnings.detail: blog_clicks fell to just 7 events, down 30.0% MoM and 56.2% YoY. If blog is a content strategy pillar, this signals low visibility, low relevance, or poor distribution—consider consolidating into homepage or location content.

biggest_opportunities.title: Rebuild organic search visibility
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic Search is the second-largest traffic channel (41.5%) but down 27.0% YoY. Conduct competitive SEO audit, refresh high-intent keywords around loan products and locations, and optimize location pages for local search to recover lost organic volume.

biggest_opportunities.title: Recover Direct traffic brand awareness
biggest_opportunities.detail: Direct traffic dropped 19.4% YoY, suggesting brand awareness or repeat visitation is eroding. Launch or refresh brand campaigns, strengthen email nurture sequences, and amplify offline marketing touchpoints (in-store QR codes, radio, local partnerships) to rebuild direct traffic.

biggest_opportunities.title: Expand location-based conversion strategy
biggest_opportunities.detail: Location pages (Knoxville +22.9% MoM, +9.5% YoY) are proving effective when optimized. Build dedicated location landing pages with localized messaging, store hours, directions (get_directions only 39 events), and testimonials; push these via Paid Search geo-targeting to drive store visits.

biggest_opportunities.title: Revitalize store_locate and locate_me events
biggest_opportunities.detail: store_locate (1,177 events, -56.3% YoY) and locate_me (537 events, -66.2% YoY) represent critical discovery moments. Audit store locator UX, ensure map accuracy, and promote store finder more prominently to recover this high-intent, location-aware audience segment.

biggest_opportunities.title: Investigate and fix engagement rate decline
biggest_opportunities.detail: Engagement rate fell 18.2 percentage points YoY despite homepage engagement remaining strong (53.3%). Audit location and secondary pages for slow load times, poor UX, or misaligned content; consider A/B testing CTA placement and value propositions to improve overall engagement quality.

recommendations.summary: March 2026 delivered strong momentum across Bedrosians' digital property, with sessions climbing 13.7% to 1,666 and engaged sessions rising 18.1% month-over-month, signaling improving content relevance and user intent match. Organic Search surged 168.2% to become the dominant channel at 61.8% of traffic, a significant shift that indicates SEO efforts are gaining traction; however, Paid Search collapsed 87.9%, suggesting budget reallocation or campaign pausing. Location-specific landing pages—particularly the Anaheim store page (+377.3% MoM) and Reno location (+890.9%)—drove disproportionate engagement and appear to be capturing local search demand effectively. Custom event performance was broadly positive, with outbound_clicks (+14.5%), mainsite_clicks (+15.0%), and product_grid_clicks (+15.6%) all climbing in line with traffic growth; notably, internal_clicks nearly doubled (+98.4%), suggesting improved site navigation or CTA visibility. Conversion-intent events show mixed signals: store_locate and locate_me grew (+24.2% and +5.7% respectively), but become_a_pro declined 13.3% and get_directions fell sharply (-70.3%), indicating potential friction in appointment or professional onboarding flows. Year-to-date, the property has accumulated 4,356 sessions across Q1 2026 with a steady upward trajectory (Jan 1,225 → Feb 1,465 → Mar 1,666), establishing a healthy growth baseline for the year ahead.

quick_wins.title: Capitalize on location-page momentum
quick_wins.detail: The Anaheim store page (210 sessions, +377.3% MoM) and Reno location (109 sessions, +890.9% MoM) are outperforming organically; ensure these pages have optimized CTAs (store_locate, get_directions, call buttons) and inventory links to convert high-intent local traffic into appointments or store visits.

quick_wins.title: Investigate and re-enable Paid Search
quick_wins.detail: Paid Search dropped 87.9% MoM to just 87 sessions; verify whether this was a deliberate pause or budget cut, and if performance was poor, audit keyword targeting and landing page relevance before reinvesting.

quick_wins.title: Fix 'not set' landing page tracking
quick_wins.detail: The '(not set)' page has 80 sessions but only 1.2% engagement, suggesting a tracking gap; audit GTM implementation to ensure all meaningful landing pages are properly tagged and attributed.

potential_warnings.title: Conversion-intent events losing traction
potential_warnings.detail: become_a_pro declined 13.3% and get_directions fell 70.3% MoM despite overall traffic growth, indicating that location/appointment conversion flows may have friction or reduced visibility that needs urgent review.

potential_warnings.title: Referral traffic collapsed
potential_warnings.detail: Referral sessions dropped 41.5% MoM to just 24 sessions; if this channel was previously meaningful, investigate whether key partner links have been removed or redirected.

potential_warnings.title: High unassigned traffic spike
potential_warnings.detail: Unassigned sessions surged 212.8% to 147 (8.8% of total), suggesting either a tracking issue with a growing traffic source or incomplete UTM parameter implementation; prioritize GTM audit to avoid blind spots in channel attribution.

biggest_opportunities.title: Double down on organic search dominance
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic Search exploded 168.2% and now represents 61.8% of traffic; invest in SEO content targeting location-based and product-grid queries (e.g. 'tile stores near me', 'slab inventory'), expand internal linking from homepage to high-performing location pages, and audit keyword gaps to accelerate this momentum.

biggest_opportunities.title: Expand local store locator and appointment flows
biggest_opportunities.detail: store_locate (+24.2%) and locate_me (+5.7%) are growing but remain low-volume (82 and 37 events); redesign homepage and location pages to more prominently surface these CTAs, and streamline the appointment booking experience to unlock conversion upside from the rising organic local search traffic.

biggest_opportunities.title: Resurrect become_a_pro program visibility
biggest_opportunities.detail: become_a_pro fell 13.3% to just 13 events despite overall engagement gains, suggesting the professional/contractor signup may be buried or unclear; audit where this CTA appears, test prominent homepage placement, and consider email or organic social prompts to re-engage contractor audience.

recommendations.summary: Budget Brakes experienced solid growth in March 2026, with sessions climbing 16.6% MoM to 5,262 and engaged sessions rising 13.2% to 2,910. Organic Search was the primary driver, surging 23.0% MoM and now accounting for 59.6% of all traffic. The engagement rate dipped slightly (−1.7 pp to 55.3%), indicating traffic growth slightly outpaced engagement gains. Custom conversion events showed strong momentum: make_an_appointment spiked 31.0% (926 events), calls climbed 11.7% (420 events), and outbound_clicks grew 7.9% (4,683 events)—all positive signals for business intent. However, store_locate collapsed 29.5% and get_directions fell 87.5%, suggesting potential friction in location-based user flows. Local landing pages (Clarksville, Murfreesboro, Knoxville, Nashville, Huntsville) drove most of the month's growth, with several posts showing 30%+ traffic increases. YTD March 2026 stands at 12,785 sessions, reflecting consistent month-over-month growth (Jan: 3,011 → Feb: 4,512 → Mar: 5,262).

quick_wins.title: Capitalize on surging appointment bookings
quick_wins.detail: make_an_appointment events jumped 31.0% to 926 in March. Ensure booking flow is frictionless and consider retargeting users who engage with high-traffic location pages (e.g., /tennessee/clarksville/823-n-2nd-st) but haven't yet converted.

quick_wins.title: Double down on organic search optimization
quick_wins.detail: Organic Search grew 23.0% MoM and now represents 59.6% of traffic. Continue building local SEO and location-specific content for Tennessee and Alabama markets—local landing pages are clearly resonating.

quick_wins.title: Investigate and fix store_locate UX
quick_wins.detail: store_locate events plummeted 29.5% MoM (403 events). Audit the store locator feature for broken functionality, poor mobile experience, or unclear CTAs that may have degraded user experience.

potential_warnings.title: Location-based navigation severely degraded
potential_warnings.detail: get_directions fell 87.5% MoM (from ~16 to 2 events) and store_locate dropped 29.5%. These are high-intent, location-critical user actions for a brake service business; their decline signals either a technical issue or UX problem that could harm conversion.

potential_warnings.title: Engagement rate declining despite traffic growth
potential_warnings.detail: Engagement rate fell 1.7 pp MoM to 55.3%, meaning incoming traffic quality is slightly weaker or users are less engaged. While overall engaged sessions grew (+13.2%), the rate suggests dilution.

potential_warnings.title: Organic Social and Paid Search remain minimal
potential_warnings.detail: Organic Social dropped 31.0% to just 20 sessions, and Paid Search contributes only 209 sessions (4.0%). These channels are underutilized and could amplify reach.

biggest_opportunities.title: Restore and optimize location-finder functionality
biggest_opportunities.detail: The 87.5% drop in get_directions and 29.5% fall in store_locate suggest users are struggling to find store information. Rebuilding a mobile-friendly locator with clear directions and call buttons could recover significant high-intent traffic and drive calls (+11.7% MoM shows demand).

biggest_opportunities.title: Expand paid and organic social strategies
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic Social is nearly invisible (20 sessions, −31.0%), and Paid Search is underinvested (209 sessions, 4.0% of mix). Allocating budget to localized social ads and search PPC targeting high-intent keywords (e.g., brake repair near [city]) could complement the strong organic foundation.

biggest_opportunities.title: Leverage local landing page momentum for lead gen
biggest_opportunities.detail: Pages like /alabama/huntsville/ (+48.3%), /tennessee/nashville/5345-charlotte-ave (+41.7%), and /tennessee/knoxville/2504-n-broadway (+39.6%) are driving strong traffic and engagement. Add targeted CTAs, appointment booking prominence, and phone numbers to capitalize on this geographic surge.

biggest_opportunities.title: Improve homepage engagement
biggest_opportunities.detail: The homepage (/) still attracts 1,147 sessions but engagement fell 3.6% MoM and sits at 57.8%. Refresh key messaging, add geolocation-based routing, and streamline CTA placement to better funnel users to appointment booking or local service pages.

recommendations.summary: Burrachos maintained relatively flat traffic in March 2026 with 3,712 sessions (+1.5% MoM), but engagement quality deteriorated: engaged sessions dropped 6.2% and engagement rate fell 3.7 percentage points to 45.6%. Organic Search remains the dominant channel at 73.2% of traffic (+9.4% MoM), while paid channels collapsed—Paid Search fell 34.6%, Organic Social fell 57.3%, and Paid Social fell 85.7%—signaling a potential budget reduction or campaign pause. On the positive side, order_pickup surged 22.1% to 1,305 events, and apply_online grew 66.7%, suggesting strong conversion momentum in key business actions. However, browse-stage events like view_menu (-12.7%) and mainsite_clicks (-9.8%) declined meaningfully, indicating fewer users are exploring menus and site content before committing to orders. Year-to-date, March represents the third consecutive month near 3,650–3,712 sessions, establishing a stable baseline for Q1 2026.

quick_wins.title: Revive Paid Search investment
quick_wins.detail: Paid Search dropped 34.6% MoM (251 sessions). Given Organic Search's strong performance (+9.4%), a modest reallocation to Paid Search—particularly for high-intent location queries—could recover lost incremental traffic with minimal creative effort.

quick_wins.title: Optimize Sun Prairie landing page engagement
quick_wins.detail: /wisconsin/sun-prairie/2691-windsor-st is the top landing page (1,018 sessions) but engagement sits at 42.5%. Simple CTA or menu visibility improvements could lift engagement without traffic acquisition spend.

quick_wins.title: Double down on order_pickup conversion funnel
quick_wins.detail: order_pickup events jumped 22.1% to 1,305—the strongest conversion signal in the dataset. Ensure this event is prominently featured in CTAs and mobile UI, and track downstream completion.

potential_warnings.title: Engagement rate declining despite traffic stability
potential_warnings.detail: Engaged sessions fell 6.2% while total sessions grew 1.5%, pushing engagement rate down 3.7 pp to 45.6%. This signals users are landing but not interacting, likely due to slower menu discovery (view_menu -12.7%) or unclear value props on landing pages.

potential_warnings.title: Paid channel collapse
potential_warnings.detail: Paid Search, Organic Social, and Paid Social combined dropped 50–85% MoM, suggesting either budget cuts, campaign pauses, or platform changes. Organic Search is now 73% of traffic—a concentration risk if organic rankings shift.

potential_warnings.title: Menu discovery friction
potential_warnings.detail: view_menu events fell 12.7% to 1,387, the steepest decline among core engagement actions. If users cannot easily find or browse menus, conversion funnels downstream (order_pickup, order_delivery) will suffer.

potential_warnings.title: Navigation usability issue
potential_warnings.detail: navigational_clicks plummeted 22.5% MoM to 186 events, suggesting poor site structure, broken internal links, or mobile navigation friction. This may explain the simultaneous drop in mainsite_clicks (-9.8%).

biggest_opportunities.title: Restore organic social presence and expand cross-network
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic Social collapsed 57.3% but Cross-network grew 66.7% (still minimal at 15 sessions). Rebuild organic social content strategy around order_pickup and apply_online events to recapture lost high-intent users at low cost.

biggest_opportunities.title: Improve Brookfield location page engagement
biggest_opportunities.detail: /wisconsin/brookfield/17530-w-bluemound-rd has 744 sessions but engagement fell 24.2% MoM and sits at 52.1%. Audit this page for broken features, slow load times, or missing local CTAs, and replicate fixes across other location pages.

biggest_opportunities.title: Capitalize on store locator momentum and fix location navigation
biggest_opportunities.detail: locate_me grew 36.4% while store_locate crashed 54%, suggesting users want quick location access but the dedicated store_locate page may be confusing or slow. Consolidate location-finding UX and promote locate_me as the primary entry point.

biggest_opportunities.title: Investigate and fix homepage engagement drop
biggest_opportunities.detail: The homepage (/) had 60.1% engagement but sessions fell 21.1% MoM. Analyze traffic sources, exit rates, and click patterns; homepage redesign or messaging refresh could reactivate this high-potential audience.

recommendations.summary: March 2026 traffic remained essentially flat month-over-month, with sessions declining 0.9% to 9,431 and engagement rate holding steady at 46.1%. However, the underlying composition of traffic and user behavior shifted meaningfully: organic search grew 6.3% and now dominates at 55.1% of sessions, while direct traffic declined 6.0%—a notable channel rebalance. Custom event data tells a more positive story, with outbound_clicks (+9.8%), internal_clicks (+10.3%), and store_locate (+15.2%) all climbing, signaling improved user intent and navigation. Location-specific landing pages (Waukegan, San Diego, Kansas City) showed particularly strong growth (26–36% MoM), though the homepage (/) remains the traffic anchor at 2,454 sessions. Year-to-date, CFSC has accumulated 28,991 sessions across Q1 2026 with a stable monthly cadence (Jan: 10,043 → Feb: 9,517 → Mar: 9,431), establishing a reliable baseline for forecasting and optimization.

quick_wins.title: Capitalize on Organic Search momentum
quick_wins.detail: Organic search grew 6.3% to 5,199 sessions and now represents 55% of traffic. Identify and double-down on the keyword clusters driving this growth via GSC data to further strengthen this high-intent channel.

quick_wins.title: Investigate and stabilize Direct traffic decline
quick_wins.detail: Direct traffic dropped 6.0% to 3,483 sessions (36.9% of total). Review branded campaign performance, email marketing CTR, and bookmark/repeat-visitor patterns to reverse this reversal and prevent further erosion.

quick_wins.title: Optimize homepage engagement
quick_wins.detail: The homepage (/) drives 2,454 sessions with 56.1% engagement, but is the only top landing page with strong volume. Audit its UX and CTA placement to push the engagement rate higher and reduce bounces.

potential_warnings.title: Paid Other channel collapse
potential_warnings.detail: Paid Other traffic fell 24.0% MoM to just 139 sessions (1.5% of total). Review campaign performance, budget allocation, and ROAS to determine if underperformance warrants reallocation or if this channel should be paused.

potential_warnings.title: Unassigned traffic growing without clarity
potential_warnings.detail: Unassigned traffic climbed 4.7% to 355 sessions. This suggests potential tracking or UTM implementation gaps; audit tag configuration to ensure accurate channel attribution and prevent further data leakage.

biggest_opportunities.title: Scale location page performance across regional markets

Custom event data reveals a content-focused audience—blog_views dominate at 371 events (+17% MoM)—but critical conversion events remain weak: view_new_inventory dropped 60% MoM to just 2 events, and schedule_service generated only 1 event in the entire month. High-engagement landing pages like /financing/how-to-afford-a-car (66.7% engagement) and /nissan/nissan-armada (42.5%) show promise, but the homepage (10.8% engagement) and check-engine-light-flashing page (36.5% engagement despite 63 sessions) indicate significant optimization opportunities.

Year-to-date, the site is tracking 1,898 sessions versus 136 in 2025—a 1295.6% increase—but the month-over-month deceleration (from 566 sessions in February to 354 in March) signals a concerning downward trend that requires immediate attention to conversion funnel health and traffic quality.

biggest_opportunities.title: Stabilize and grow direct traffic intent quality

recommendations.summary: March 2026 delivered strong growth momentum for FRE, with sessions climbing 53.4% month-over-month and 110.4% year-over-year to 37,184 total sessions. However, engagement rate declined 5.2 percentage points MoM and 13.4 percentage points YoY despite engaged sessions growing 37.1% MoM, signaling that traffic volume is outpacing engagement quality—a typical pattern when new, less-qualified traffic enters the funnel. All six custom conversion events grew substantially, with shop_now (+81.4% YoY) and mainsite_clicks (+78.6% YoY) showing the strongest year-over-year gains, indicating genuine intent-driven behavior. Direct traffic surged 64.1% MoM and 123.0% YoY to dominate at 59.3% of sessions, while organic search grew a more modest 19.8% MoM despite holding 34.1% share. Unassigned traffic spiked 910.3% MoM, which requires immediate investigation for tracking gaps. The homepage (/) remains the traffic engine with 65.1% engagement, but state-level landing pages (/california, /texas, /florida) are emerging as high-growth engines with strong engagement rates, particularly /florida (+165.1% MoM, 46.5% engagement). recommendations.summary: March 2026 shows a sharp traffic deceleration after two exceptionally strong months: sessions dropped 61% MoM to 12,833, though YoY growth remains dramatic at +1824% versus March 2025's 667 sessions. Direct traffic dominates at 97.2% of sessions and is responsible for the MoM decline (-61.6%), while organic search remained relatively flat (-2.7% MoM). Conversion events paint a mixed picture: apply_now events declined 19.1% MoM to 93, but navigational_clicks surged 130.5% to 136, suggesting users are exploring the site rather than completing applications. quick_wins.detail: Direct traffic fell 61.6% MoM despite comprising 97.2% of total sessions. Investigate whether this reflects campaign pause, expired bookmarks, or organic decline in brand searches. Cross-reference with any paid/owned media spend changes in March.

quick_wins.detail: Nine of the top 10 landing pages are location-specific URLs (e.g., /north-carolina/durham/...) with zero engagement. Combine low-traffic location pages, improve internal linking from homepage, and ensure mobile usability to unlock engagement on this high-volume but cold traffic.

quick_wins.detail: 61 sessions landed on '(not set)' in March (+45.2% MoM), with zero engagement. This indicates a tracking gap or misconfiguration. Audit GA4 event filters and page path canonicalization to recapture lost visibility.

potential_warnings.title: apply_now conversions declining despite traffic growth YoY
potential_warnings.detail: apply_now events dropped 19.1% MoM to 93, and while YoY growth is positive (+13.4%), the MoM decline combined with 61% session drop suggests conversion rate may be contracting. Monitor this closely as it is a primary business outcome.

recommendations.summary: March 2026 showed modest month-over-month recovery, with sessions up 14.6% to 5,674 and engaged sessions climbing 21.4% to 1,397, signaling improved visitor quality despite a challenging year-over-year environment. However, the YoY picture remains deeply concerning: sessions are down 45.1% and engaged sessions have collapsed 74.7% compared to March 2025, reflecting a sustained traffic crisis that has persisted throughout Q1 2026. The engagement rate improved slightly (24.6%, +1.4 pp MoM) but remains 28.7 percentage points below March 2025. Direct traffic now dominates at 76.7% of sessions and is up 164.2% YoY—a counterintuitive bright spot that likely reflects branded/returning visitor behavior—while organic search has cratered 86.3% YoY to just 1,175 sessions. Conversion-adjacent events tell a mixed story: outbound clicks and phone calls both grew MoM (+14.6% and +22.0%) but remain devastated YoY (-72.7% and -81.4%), while location-finding events (store_locate, locate_me) show modest growth momentum. The Louisiana Harvey location (4401-peters-rd) remains the engagement star at 69.8%, but the homepage engagement at 40.8% is dragging relative to its 634-session volume. Year-to-date 2026 is tracking 38.9% below 2025, and unless organic search recovers or paid acquisition activates, this trajectory will not reverse.

potential_warnings.detail: Phone calls (194 events) and outbound clicks (2,340 events) are down 81.4% and 72.7% YoY respectively, indicating both awareness and intent are severely impaired. Even though MoM growth is positive, absolute volumes remain far below prior-year performance.

potential_warnings.detail: Organic search has fallen from a much larger baseline to just 1,175 sessions (20.7% of total) in March 2026, representing an 86.3% YoY decline. This is Pull-A-Part's primary customer acquisition channel and its collapse is unsustainable.

biggest_opportunities.detail: /louisiana/harvey/4401-peters-rd leads with 69.8% engagement and 579 sessions; analyze its on-page structure, CTA placement, and content strategy, then replicate this template across other location pages (especially Charlotte, Greensboro) to lift engagement across the portfolio.

biggest_opportunities.detail: Calls events (194) are up 22% MoM but down 81.4% YoY; implement click-to-call buttons, local phone numbers per location, and prominent call CTAs on all location pages to increase conversion from visitor to phone inquiry.

recommendations.summary: March 2026 delivered strong year-over-year growth but showed early signs of deceleration compared to the momentum of February. Total sessions reached 545,947, down 3.6% month-over-month but up 137.3% versus March 2025, reflecting sustained but plateauing traffic expansion across Q1 2026. Engagement quality improved significantly: engaged sessions grew 12.1% MoM and engagement rate climbed 3.2 percentage points to 22.8%, suggesting better visitor intent despite lower overall volume. Custom conversion events showed mixed signals—outbound_clicks and navigational_clicks remained strong (up 16.0% and 18.9% MoM respectively), but more critical drop events revealed explosive volatility: drops_shop surged 133.3% MoM (126 events) and drops_sign_up doubled MoM (42 events), indicating either major campaign activity or potential friction in conversion funnels that warrant investigation. Direct traffic dominated at 59% of sessions with a staggering 1,693.6% YoY lift, while organic search held steady at 34.6% with minimal YoY change (+0.8%), and paid search accelerated sharply at 79.3% MoM growth. The homepage (/) captured 41% engagement and grew 64.1% MoM, yet 5,439 sessions fell into '(not set)' with only 1% engagement, pointing to tracking gaps or unoptimized entry points. Year-to-date, Q1 2026 is tracking 137.7% ahead of 2025, but the MoM deceleration from February's +175.7% growth to March's +137.3% warrants monitoring to ensure the uptrend does not plateau further.

quick_wins.title: Resolve '(not set)' landing page tracking
quick_wins.detail: 5,439 sessions with only 1% engagement are unattributed; audit GA4 page view configuration and UTM parameters to properly categorize this traffic and unlock visibility into user behavior patterns.

quick_wins.detail: The homepage / is the top landing page with 9,965 sessions and 41% engagement; test above-the-fold CTAs, clearer value propositions, and mobile UX to push engagement rate closer to location-specific pages (e.g., Brooklyn landing page at 67.7%).

quick_wins.title: Investigate and scale paid search momentum
quick_wins.detail: Paid search sessions grew 79.3% MoM (23,177 sessions) and 14.6% YoY; audit top-performing keywords and ad copy, then increase budget allocation to capitalize on this accelerating channel.

potential_warnings.title: Month-over-month session decline signals growth plateau
potential_warnings.detail: Overall sessions fell 3.6% MoM after explosive February growth (+175.7% YoY); while YoY performance remains strong, the MoM deceleration suggests campaign lift may be waning or market saturation is beginning—monitor April closely to confirm whether this is seasonal or structural.

potential_warnings.title: Organic search growth has stalled year-over-year
potential_warnings.detail: Organic search sessions grew only 0.8% YoY despite 34.6% of total traffic; this suggests SEO efforts are not scaling with overall site growth or competitive pressure is increasing—audit keyword rankings and SERP visibility to identify lost opportunities.

potential_warnings.title: Direct traffic spike lacks explainability
potential_warnings.detail: Direct traffic exploded 1,693.6% YoY and fell 17.7% MoM; the extreme YoY growth combined with MoM decline indicates either one-time brand campaign lift in March 2025 baseline or misattribution issues—clarify whether this is true direct traffic or untagged campaigns.

potential_warnings.detail: drops_shop (126 events, +133.3% MoM) and drops_sign_up (42 events, +110.0% MoM) both spiked sharply; if these represent abandonment points rather than intentional exits, they suggest checkout or registration friction that is growing month-over-month and requires UX audit.

potential_warnings.title: Organic social near-collapse
potential_warnings.detail: Organic social traffic fell 88.9% to just 3 sessions. While small in absolute terms, this channel's severe contraction may indicate algorithm changes, reduced posting, or loss of audience interest.

biggest_opportunities.title: Capitalize on geo-targeted GLP-1 content performance
biggest_opportunities.detail: Pages like /glp-1/hawaii (+220% MoM) and /glp-1/alabama (75% engagement) show strong product-market fit. Expand the state/geo targeting strategy across all 50 states with localized provider comparisons and SEO optimization to drive both organic search and paid acquisition.

biggest_opportunities.title: Rebuild direct traffic through retention and email
biggest_opportunities.detail: Direct traffic accounts for 65.6% of sessions but dropped 21.1% MoM. Implement email nurture campaigns, retargeting, and loyalty incentives to re-engage lapsed users and stabilize this critical channel.

biggest_opportunities.title: Audit and repair the provider discovery funnel
biggest_opportunities.detail: weight_loss_provider_views_orderlymeds and show_providers_form_submit declined 27.9% and 77.8% respectively. A full funnel audit—from landing to provider comparison to form submission—is needed to identify where users are dropping and why conversion is stalling.

biggest_opportunities.title: Re-energize organic search strategy
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic search fell 27.7% despite lifestyle content pages performing well. Conduct keyword research around high-intent GLP-1 and telehealth terms, optimize top-performing pages for linked domains and backlinks, and revisit technical SEO to recover visibility.

recommendations.summary: March 2026 brought a significant MoM traffic decline of 18.9% (344 sessions vs 424 in February), continuing a deceleration trend that started in January (784 sessions). However, the YoY picture remains exceptionally strong, with March 2026 up 437.5% versus March 2025 (64 sessions), and Q1 2026 delivering 1,552 sessions against just 154 in Q1 2025—a 907.8% increase. Direct traffic dominates at 89.5% of sessions and grew 1,300% YoY, though it declined 19.2% MoM, suggesting unsustainable organic growth has plateaued. Engagement rate improved 4.0 percentage points MoM to 20.9%, but remains 49.4 pp below March 2025 levels, indicating lower-quality traffic. Custom conversion events show mixed signals: store_locate surged 36.8% MoM and 271.4% YoY (26 events, now the third-highest conversion driver), mainsite_clicks grew 16.7% MoM, and shop_now doubled; conversely, calls collapsed 61.5% MoM and 54.5% YoY (5 events), and locate_me dropped 85.7% MoM, signaling potential friction in high-intent conversion paths. The homepage (/) remains the traffic engine (186 sessions, 20.4% engagement) with stellar 708.7% YoY growth, while location-specific pages like /kentucky/bowling-green show strong engagement (100%) but minimal volume.

quick_wins.title: Investigate homepage engagement gap
quick_wins.detail: The homepage drives 54% of sessions but only 20.4% engagement—well below the 38.6% average. Audit CTA clarity, form friction, and mobile UX to unlock conversion potential from 148 unengaged homepage visitors monthly.

quick_wins.title: Prioritize store_locate optimization
quick_wins.detail: store_locate events surged 36.8% MoM and 271.4% YoY to become the third-highest conversion event (26). Ensure the locator feature is prominent on high-traffic pages and mobile-responsive to sustain momentum.

quick_wins.title: Analyze direct traffic source attribution
quick_wins.detail: Direct traffic grew 1,300% YoY but dropped 19.2% MoM; this anomalous growth suggests possible tracking issues (bookmarks, untagged campaigns, or domain migrations). Audit UTM parameters and referral data to identify true source.

potential_warnings.title: Session decline accelerating month-over-month
potential_warnings.detail: Sessions dropped 18.9% MoM from February to March, continuing a downward trend from January's 784 sessions. This suggests traffic acquisition may be unsustainable without channel diversification or organic search investment.

potential_warnings.title: Phone calls conversion plummeting
potential_warnings.detail: Calls events collapsed 61.5% MoM (from 13 to 5) and 54.5% YoY, indicating potential barriers to phone contact—missing/unclear phone CTAs, slow page load, or mobile usability issues. This is a high-intent conversion path worth investigating.

potential_warnings.title: Organic search traffic declining YoY
potential_warnings.detail: Organic search fell 15.4% YoY and 13.2% MoM to just 33 sessions (9.6% of traffic), while direct traffic artificially inflates the mix. Long-term SEO health depends on reversing this trend.

potential_warnings.title: Engagement quality regression despite rate improvement
potential_warnings.detail: While engagement rate rose 4.0 pp MoM to 20.9%, it is still down 49.4 pp YoY—meaning March 2026 traffic is significantly lower-quality than March 2025, despite higher absolute sessions.

biggest_opportunities.title: Restore and grow organic search channel
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic search represents only 9.6% of traffic and is declining YoY. Investing in SEO for location-based keywords (e.g., 'radon testing [city]') can diversify away from direct traffic and capture high-intent search demand.

biggest_opportunities.title: Scale location-specific landing pages
biggest_opportunities.detail: /kentucky/bowling-green achieved 100% engagement with only 7 sessions; replicating this template and promoting location pages across service areas could convert the 287 low-engagement homepage visitors and location-page visitors into higher-value leads.

biggest_opportunities.title: Unblock phone call conversion path
biggest_opportunities.detail: With calls dropping 61.5% MoM, audit mobile CTAs, phone number visibility, and click-to-call functionality. Even a 50% recovery would add 4–5 calls/month from existing traffic—high-value outcomes for radon testing.

biggest_opportunities.title: Reactivate locate_me feature or understand disengagement
biggest_opportunities.detail: locate_me fell 85.7% MoM to just 1 event. Determine if this feature is broken, hidden, or no longer resonates with users; if it was a strategic tool for on-site localization, restoring visibility could unlock a new conversion vector.

recommendations.summary: March 2026 saw a dramatic 61% month-over-month session decline (4,502 sessions vs. 11,549 in February), continuing a decelerating trend that started in January's peak of 23,220 sessions. However, the quality of remaining traffic improved meaningfully: engaged sessions rose 31.3% MoM and engagement rate climbed 10.9 percentage points to 15.6%, suggesting Weinstock is retaining and converting higher-intent visitors. Year-over-year, March 2026 still delivered 1,918.8% more sessions than March 2025 (223), confirming sustained growth relative to prior year. Direct traffic dominates at 88.4% of sessions and grew explosively YoY (+13,173%), though it declined 64.4% MoM in line with overall traffic seasonality. Among custom conversion events, book_now surged 25% MoM and 204.3% YoY (70 events), calls exploded 1,175% YoY (102 events), and outbound_clicks remained steady with modest 5.5% MoM growth. The homepage (/) remains the engine, driving 550 sessions with an exceptional 74.9% engagement rate and +131.1% MoM growth. The YTD 2026 trajectory shows 39,271 sessions across Q1—a 3,982% lift over 2025—but monthly deceleration from January through March signals either seasonal normalization or the tail end of a campaign push requiring attention to sustain momentum.

quick_wins.title: Capitalize on homepage engagement momentum
quick_wins.detail: The homepage (/) is performing exceptionally with 74.9% engagement rate and +131.1% MoM growth (550 sessions). Test adding prominent CTAs for book_now and calls events to convert this high-engagement traffic into measurable conversions.

quick_wins.title: Investigate and fix '(not set)' landing page leakage
quick_wins.detail: 165 sessions landed on '(not set)' with only 0.6% engagement—a 37.5% MoM increase in unmeasured traffic. This likely reflects tracking misconfiguration; audit UTM parameters and internal linking to reclaim attribution and engagement visibility.

quick_wins.title: Double down on high-intent conversion events
quick_wins.detail: book_now (+25% MoM, 70 events) and calls (+5.2% MoM, 102 events) are the strongest conversion signals and remain growth drivers YoY. Ensure these events are prominently linked from top landing pages and optimized for mobile.

potential_warnings.title: Sharp month-over-month session decline signals deceleration
potential_warnings.detail: Sessions fell 61% from February to March (11,549 → 4,502), extending a three-month downtrend that began in January. While engagement quality improved, absolute traffic volume is concerning and may indicate campaign spend reduction, seasonal drop-off, or audience fatigue requiring intervention.

potential_warnings.title: Organic search remains negligible despite 98% YoY growth
potential_warnings.detail: Organic search generated only 313 sessions (7% of traffic) and shows minimal MoM movement (+2.6%), while direct traffic dominates at 88.4%. Heavy reliance on direct traffic suggests limited organic SEO traction or paid/branded campaign dependency, creating vulnerability if direct channels underperform.

potential_warnings.title: Locational landing pages show low engagement despite targeting specificity
potential_warnings.detail: Pages like /south-carolina/long-creek (8 sessions, 0% engaged) and /florida/west-palm-beach/tn-h1b-visas (8 sessions, 12.5% engaged) attract minimal traffic with poor conversion signals. These may represent low-demand geo/visa combinations or ineffective SEO targeting.

biggest_opportunities.title: Rebuild organic search channel to reduce direct traffic dependency
biggest_opportunities.detail: With 88.4% of traffic coming from direct and only 7% from organic search, investing in SEO for high-intent visa and immigration keywords could diversify the channel mix and reduce reliance on paid or branded campaigns. Organic search already showed 98% YoY growth, proving traction is possible.

biggest_opportunities.title: Analyze and reactivate February's traffic sources to reverse decline
biggest_opportunities.detail: February delivered 11,549 sessions vs. March's 4,502—a 61% drop. Identify which campaigns, channels, or content drove February's peak and re-allocate budget or refresh creative to sustain that volume level into Q2.

biggest_opportunities.title: Optimize location-specific pages for high-engagement conversion funnels
biggest_opportunities.detail: Pages like /minnesota/minneapolis/green-cards-employment-based-immigrants (66.7% engagement) and /georgia/folkston (60% engagement) show strong engagement signals despite low volume. Scale these high-intent pages via targeted ads or internal promotion to convert location-based intent into book_now and calls events.

recommendations.summary: March 2026 delivered mixed signals: total sessions dropped 73.0% MoM to 11,912, a sharp reversal after February's 1,443% YoY surge, yet YoY growth remains strong at +214.0%. Engagement rate improved 12.1 percentage points MoM to 20.1%, suggesting the traffic decline filtered out low-quality visitors. Custom conversion events showed resilience, with outbound_clicks (+14.0% MoM, +24.7% YoY), mainsite_clicks (+9.2% MoM, +51.2% YoY), and shop_now (+8.2% MoM, +61.2% YoY) all growing despite the session collapse. Direct traffic cratered 80.0% MoM but remains the dominant channel at 68.4% of sessions; organic search grew 8.1% MoM and now represents 31.5% of traffic. Location-specific landing pages (e.g., /texas/lamesa/802-n-4th-st, /ohio/carrollton/701-canton-rd-nw) are driving high engagement (44–57%), while the homepage engagement rate of 38.5% trails overall momentum. Year-to-date, Q1 2026 is tracking 636.3% ahead of Q1 2025, but the sharp March deceleration warrants investigation into whether February's spike was campaign-driven or unsustainable.

quick_wins.title: Investigate Direct traffic cliff
quick_wins.detail: Direct traffic dropped 80.0% MoM despite +879.8% YoY growth; confirm whether a campaign, referral source, or tracking issue caused the February spike and subsequent collapse. This is critical given Direct represents 68.4% of March sessions.

quick_wins.title: Expand high-engagement location pages
quick_wins.detail: Pages like /texas/lamesa/802-n-4th-st (55.6% engagement, +89.5% MoM) and /ohio/carrollton/701-canton-rd-nw (56.7% engagement) significantly outperform the homepage (38.5%). Replicate their content/UX template across other location pages.

quick_wins.title: Double down on shop_now conversions
quick_wins.detail: shop_now events grew +8.2% MoM and +61.2% YoY despite session decline, indicating strong product-market fit. Ensure conversion funnel is optimized and consider A/B testing CTA placement and messaging.

potential_warnings.title: Dramatic MoM session collapse requires root cause analysis
potential_warnings.detail: Sessions fell from 44,086 (Feb) to 11,912 (Mar), a 73% drop. While YoY is still +214%, this sharp reversal suggests February may have been an anomaly (possibly campaign-driven). Verify traffic source attribution and confirm no tracking issues.

potential_warnings.title: store_locate and locate_me conversions collapsing
potential_warnings.detail: store_locate dropped 2.1% MoM and -99.6% YoY (near-total collapse), and locate_me fell 5.0% MoM and -62.3% YoY. These are likely high-intent local business events; investigate whether feature deprecation, UX changes, or competitive shifts are driving abandonment.

potential_warnings.title: Homepage engagement lagging peer location pages
potential_warnings.detail: Homepage engagement rate of 38.5% is 6–18 percentage points below top location pages. While homepage drives 551 sessions (44.5% of traffic), its underperformance suggests missed optimization opportunities for a critical funnel entry point.

biggest_opportunities.title: Recover store_locate and locate_me functionality
biggest_opportunities.detail: These events have virtually disappeared YoY despite being high-intent local business actions. Audit the user experience, mobile responsiveness, and feature visibility; restoring even 50% of prior performance could unlock significant local traffic and conversions.

biggest_opportunities.title: Scale organic search channel
biggest_opportunities.detail: Organic search grew 8.1% MoM and +26.7% YoY and now drives 3,758 sessions (31.5% of traffic). With Direct volatility evident, investing in SEO (keyword expansion, location pages, technical SEO) could build a more stable, scalable traffic foundation.

biggest_opportunities.title: Capitalize on location-page conversion strength
biggest_opportunities.detail: Location-specific pages are generating 44–57% engagement rates and driving shop_now and get_directions events. Systematically expand coverage of underserved locations and optimize template consistency to compound this trend.
